title: Security: Add rate limiting to all authentication endpoints to prevent brute force attacks
vulnerability_description: Authentication endpoints have no rate limiting. An attacker can make unlimited requests to brute force passwords or enumerate registered emails.

acceptance_criteria.given: any IP makes more than 10 requests to POST /api/v1/auth/login within 15 minutes
acceptance_criteria.then: the response is 429 Too Many Requests with a Retry-After header

acceptance_criteria.given: rate limit counters are stored in Redis and Redis becomes unavailable
acceptance_criteria.then: requests are allowed through — rate limiting degrades gracefully

acceptance_criteria.given: more than 5 registration attempts are made from one IP in 1 hour
acceptance_criteria.then: it returns 429 with a Retry-After header

definition_of_done: Rate limiting test passes on all three auth endpoints
Redis unavailability graceful degradation test passes
Retry-After header present in all 429 responses
agent_instructions: Write the test that demonstrates missing rate limiting FIRST. Confirm it fails. Then implement. Confirm it passes.
